natural: one of the strongest everyday Thai swears: as an interjection 'fuck!/shit!', or aimed at a person 'evil bastard/piece of shit'
whoUses: heard constantly in angry or intense male and mixed-company speech; taboo in polite settings
usageContext: venting anger, shock, or contempt; extremely offensive when directed at someone
warningNotes: Very strong. Even the plain word for the lizard (ตัวเงินตัวทอง is the polite substitute) is avoided because of this swear. Never use toward a person; understand it, don't say it.

examples.text: เหี้ย ลืมกุญแจไว้ในรถ
examples.romaji: hîa, luem kun-jae wái nai rót
examples.translation: Fuck, I left the keys in the car.

phrase: สัตว์ / สัส
natural: 'you animal / beast' as an insult, or a heavy intensifier tacked on the end ('...as fuck'); สัส is the slangy respelling
whoUses: common in heated informal speech, especially among young men online and offline
usageContext: insulting someone's behavior, or intensifying: 'โคตรแพงสัส' = 'fucking expensive'
warningNotes: The neutral word สัตว์ 'animal' is fine in normal contexts (e.g. 'สวนสัตว์' = zoo); it only becomes a swear as an insult or intensifier. สัส is unmistakably vulgar.

examples.text: รถติดโคตรสัส

natural: the vulgar word for penis; also a raw interjection of anger or a dismissive 'bullshit'
whoUses: crude informal speech, mostly male; strongly taboo in mixed or polite company
usageContext: insult, angry outburst, or crude reference to the anatomy
saferAlternatives: (polite/clinical) อวัยวะเพศชาย
warningNotes: Highly offensive. Do not use. Included for comprehension only.

examples.text: พูดอะไรของมึง ควย
examples.translation: What the hell are you talking about — bullshit.

natural: the crudest word for the female genitals; a strong obscenity
whoUses: very crude speech; deeply taboo in almost all settings
usageContext: crude anatomical reference or vulgar insult
saferAlternatives: (polite/clinical) อวัยวะเพศหญิง
warningNotes: Extremely vulgar and offensive. Do not use. Recognition only.

examples.text: เขาพูดคำว่า หี ออกสื่อไม่ได้
examples.romaji: kháo phûut kham wâa hǐi ɔ̀ɔk sùee mâi dâi
examples.translation: That word (cunt) can't be said on air.

natural: the vulgar verb 'to fuck'; also in aggressive curses like 'เย็ดแม่' (a severe insult)
whoUses: the crudest, most aggressive speech; near-total taboo
usageContext: extreme anger, sexual crudeness, or fighting words; 'เย็ดแม่' targets someone's mother and can start fights
saferAlternatives: (polite) มีเพศสัมพันธ์
warningNotes: Among the most severe in Thai, especially in mother-directed curses. Never use. Recognition only.

examples.text: อย่าพูดคำหยาบแบบนั้น มันแรงมาก
examples.romaji: yàa phûut kham yàap bàep nán, man raeng mâak
examples.translation: Don't say that swear — it's extremely strong.

literal: (reduced form of มัน 'it' fused with a curse)
natural: a vulgar emphatic 'damn / fucking' dropped into a sentence for force
whoUses: very common in relaxed male and young-people speech; not for polite settings
usageContext: emphasis or irritation mid-sentence; 'แม่งโคตรเซ็ง' = 'this is fucking annoying'
saferAlternatives: น่าเบื่อจัง
warningNotes: Milder than เหี้ย/ควย but still clearly vulgar. Fine to understand; avoid producing it with strangers, elders, or at work.

examples.text: รอมาตั้งนาน แม่งไม่มาซะที
examples.romaji: raaw maa tâng naan, mâeng mâi maa sá thii
examples.translation: Waited forever and the damn thing still isn't here.

literal: derogatory prefix for males (ไอ้) / for females (อี)
natural: a contemptuous name-prefix, like 'that damn ...' before a person's name or a noun (ไอ้เหี้ย, อีบ้า); attaches the speaker's scorn
whoUses: angry or crude speech; also, confusingly, used affectionately among very close male friends
usageContext: insulting or dismissing someone; อี toward a woman is especially offensive
warningNotes: Directed at a woman, อี is a serious insult. Among tight male friends ไอ้ can be joking ('ไอ้เพื่อน'), but a learner will misjudge the line — avoid producing it.

examples.text: ไอ้นี่มันโกหกตลอด
examples.translation: This damn guy lies all the time.

natural: a vulgar intensifier 'as hell / fucking / super' placed before adjectives; 'โคตรดี' = 'fucking great'
whoUses: very widespread in casual speech, especially younger people; not for formal settings
usageContext: amplifying anything, good or bad; its 'ancestry' sense makes it crude to elders
warningNotes: Extremely common but vulgar; touching on ancestry it can offend elders. Understand it; use มาก/สุดๆ instead.

examples.text: หนังเรื่องนี้โคตรสนุก

literal: to be ruined / go to ruin
natural: 'goddamn / all hell / totally screwed'; an exclamation of disaster or a heavy intensifier
whoUses: heated informal speech; the variant ชิบหาย is softened slightly
usageContext: reacting to a disaster, or intensifying: 'แพงฉิบหาย' = 'ridiculously expensive'
warningNotes: Strongly vulgar. Fine for comprehension; don't use in polite or professional company.

examples.text: ฉิบหายแล้ว ลืมส่งงาน
examples.translation: Oh hell, I forgot to submit the work.

natural: the crude/intimate pronoun pair for 'you' and 'I'; warm and normal between close friends, but rude and aggressive toward anyone else
whoUses: extremely common among close friends and within tight peer groups of all ages; a fighting register with strangers
usageContext: relaxed talk between best friends signals closeness; aimed at a stranger, elder, or superior it's a deliberate insult
saferAlternatives: คุณ / ผม / ดิฉัน
warningNotes: Context is everything: bonding among friends, but provocative or contemptuous otherwise. A learner should recognize it and default to เธอ/ฉัน or คุณ/ผม.

examples.text: มึงจะไปไหน กูรอตั้งนาน
examples.romaji: mueng jà pai nǎi, kuu raaw tâng naan
examples.translation: Where are you going? I've been waiting ages. (between close friends)
